parameters.content:
## 📰 News Scraper — RSS → Translate → Publish

Fetches articles from your RSS feeds, dedupes them, enriches them with an
image + full text from the article page, makes every article **bilingual
(Arabic ⇄ English)** with an LLM, and POSTs the result to your own API.

### Quick start
1. Open **⚙️ Config** → set your API endpoint + auth.
2. Open **📰 Prepare Sources** → add your feeds & category IDs.
3. Select your **OpenAI credential** on the *Translate* node.
4. Hit **Execute workflow** to test, then enable the *Schedule* trigger.

📖 Full docs: see `README.md` and `docs/` in the repository.

## ✏️ EDIT THESE TWO NODES

**⚙️ Config** — API endpoint, auth header, item limits, placeholder image.

**📰 Prepare Sources** — your RSS feeds and the slug → category-ID map
for your backend. Everything else works without changes.

## 🔁 Per-source loop

*Per Source* processes one feed at a time (isolates failures: one broken
feed never kills the run). *Fetch RSS* and most downstream nodes use
**continue-on-error**, so the loop always completes.

*Build Articles* takes the newest N items, skips items already posted
(dedupe via workflow static data) and emits a `_skip` marker when a feed
yields nothing — *Has Articles?* routes those straight back to the loop.

## 🖼️ Enrich + 🌐 Translate

*Fetch Article Page* downloads the article HTML (never errors, 8s timeout).
*Resolve Image* picks the best image (RSS → og:image → twitter:image) and
extracts the full article text when it beats the feed summary.

*Translate* (OpenAI, structured output) fills ONLY the empty language
fields — filled fields pass through unchanged. If the LLM fails, the
article still publishes via fallbacks in *Build Payload*.

## 🚀 Publish

*Build Payload* assembles the final bilingual object (guaranteed
non-empty fields). *POST Article* sends it to `Config.apiUrl` with your
auth header. *Mark Seen* records the article as published **only on a
2xx response** — failures are retried on the next run.

Payload contract → `docs/configuration.md`.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.notes: Manual trigger for testing. For production, enable the Schedule trigger below instead.

nodes.name: Schedule
nodes.notes: Optional scheduled run (default: every 6 hours). Enable this node and activate the workflow for production. IMPORTANT: the dedupe store (workflow static data) only persists for active/scheduled runs, not manual test runs.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: ⚙️ EDIT ME — central configuration: API endpoint, auth header, per-feed item limit, text length caps, placeholder image. Every other node reads from here.

nodes.name: Prepare Sources
nodes.notes: 📰 EDIT ME — define your RSS feeds (SOURCES) and the slug → numeric category-ID map for your backend (CATEGORIES). Ships with 5 public example feeds.

nodes.name: Per Source
nodes.notes: Loops over sources one at a time so a single broken feed can't break the whole run. Output 1 = 'all done', Output 2 = next source.

nodes.name: Done
nodes.notes: Final summary item once every source has been processed.

nodes.name: Fetch RSS
nodes.notes: Reads the current source's feed. customFields exposes enclosure/media tags so Build Articles can pick a feed image. Continues on error so dead feeds don't stop the run.

nodes.name: Build Articles
nodes.notes: Normalizes RSS items into article objects: newest N items, dedupe against previously-posted IDs, fills source-language fields and leaves the other language empty for Translate.

nodes.name: Has Articles?
nodes.notes: TRUE branch = feed produced nothing new (skip marker) → loop to next source. FALSE branch = real articles → enrich & publish.

nodes.name: Skip Source
nodes.notes: Records WHY a source was skipped (no_rss_items / all_already_seen / no_usable_url) and hands control back to the loop.

nodes.name: Fetch Article Page
nodes.notes: Downloads the article's HTML page (8s timeout, never errors) so we can extract an og:image and the full article text. If it fails, the feed summary is used instead.

nodes.name: Resolve Image
nodes.notes: Picks the best image (RSS image → og:image → twitter:image) and upgrades the description to the full page text when it's longer than the feed summary.

values.content:
You localize news articles between Arabic and English.
You receive a JSON object with six fields: title_ar, title_en, short_description_ar, short_description_en, description_ar, description_en.
Some fields are filled (in their respective language) and some are empty strings.
For every EMPTY field, translate from its non-empty counterpart in the other language (e.g. empty title_ar -> translate title_en into Arabic).
For every NON-EMPTY field, return it UNCHANGED. Do not retranslate, rewrite, summarise, or "improve" filled fields.
Never leave a field empty if its counterpart in the other language has text — always provide the translation.
Preserve named entities (people, places, brands). Do not add commentary. Output exactly the six fields, all as strings.

nodes.name: Translate
nodes.notes: 🔑 SETUP REQUIRED — attach your own OpenAI credential here. Fills ONLY the empty language fields (structured JSON output); never rewrites filled ones. Swap the model if you prefer another. Continues on error: Build Payload has fallbacks.

nodes.name: Build Payload
nodes.notes: Merges the translation into the article and builds the final API payload. Defensive: guarantees every text field is non-empty even if the LLM failed.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ category_id: $json.category_id, source_url: $json.source_url, published_at: $json.published_at, title_ar: $json.title_ar, title_en: $json.title_en, short_description_ar: $json.short_description_ar, short_description_en: $json.short_description_en, description_ar: $json.description_ar, description_en: $json.description_en, image: $json.image, status: $json.status }) }}

nodes.name: POST Article
nodes.notes: Sends the article to YOUR endpoint — URL and auth header both come from the Config node, nothing is hardcoded here. fullResponse + neverError let Mark Seen inspect the status code.

nodes.name: Mark Seen
nodes.notes: Marks the article as published in the dedupe store only on a 2xx response, then loops back for the next source. Failed posts are retried next run.
